---
title: "Sample"
output: html_document
---
```{rsetup }
# ============================================================
# Bulk RNA-seq analysis up to PCA
# Using nf-core/rnaseq STAR-Salmon output
# ============================================================

suppressPackageStartupMessages({
  library(SummarizedExperiment)
  library(DESeq2)
  library(ggplot2)
})

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Set working directory
# Change this path if needed
# ------------------------------------------------------------

setwd("Results/star_salmon")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Load merged Salmon gene-level SummarizedExperiment object
# ------------------------------------------------------------

se <- readRDS("/Users/preddy/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/salmon.merged.gene.SummarizedExperiment.rds")

# Check object
se
assayNames(se)
dim(se)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Extract raw gene counts
# ------------------------------------------------------------

counts_mat <- assay(se)

remove_samples <- c("A1", "D1", "G1")

counts_mat <- counts_mat[, !colnames(counts_mat) %in% remove_samples]

cat("Samples after removing A1, D1, G1:\n")
print(colnames(counts_mat))


# Check counts
counts_mat[1:5, 1:5]

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Create sample metadata from sample names
# ------------------------------------------------------------

sample_names <- colnames(counts_mat)

sample_info <- data.frame(
  sample_id = sample_names,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

# Example sample name:
# OPA1_WT_CD_1
# OPA1_KO_HFD_12
# ATF4_WT_CD_13
# AO_KO_CD_25

sample_info$gene_group <- sub("^(OPA1|ATF4|AO)_.*", "\\1", sample_info$sample_id)
sample_info$status <- sub("^(OPA1|ATF4|AO)_(WT|KO)_.*", "\\2", sample_info$sample_id)
sample_info$diet <- sub("^(OPA1|ATF4|AO)_(WT|KO)_(CD|HFD)_.*", "\\3", sample_info$sample_id)

# Remove final replicate/sample number or letter-number part
sample_info$condition <- sub("_[^_]+$", "", sample_info$sample_id)

# Convert to factors
sample_info$gene_group <- factor(sample_info$gene_group)
sample_info$status <- factor(sample_info$status, levels = c("WT", "KO"))
sample_info$diet <- factor(sample_info$diet, levels = c("CD", "HFD"))
sample_info$condition <- factor(sample_info$condition)

rownames(sample_info) <- sample_info$sample_id

# Check metadata
print(sample_info)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 5. Make sure sample order matches count matrix
# ------------------------------------------------------------

counts_mat <- counts_mat[, sample_info$sample_id]

stopifnot(all(colnames(counts_mat) == rownames(sample_info)))

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Filter low-expression genes
# Keep genes with count >= 10 in at least 3 samples
# ------------------------------------------------------------

keep <- rowSums(counts_mat >= 10) >= 3
counts_filtered <- counts_mat[keep, ]

cat("Genes before filtering:", nrow(counts_mat), "\n")
cat("Genes after filtering:", nrow(counts_filtered), "\n")

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 7. Create DESeq2 object
# For PCA, condition-based design is fine
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(
  countData = round(counts_filtered),
  colData = sample_info,
  design = ~ condition
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 8. Run DESeq2 normalization
# ------------------------------------------------------------

dds <- DESeq(dds)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 9. Variance stabilizing transformation
# ------------------------------------------------------------

vsd <- vst(dds, blind = FALSE)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 10. PCA plot colored by condition
# ------------------------------------------------------------

output_dir <- "/Users/preddy/Desktop/Lab_Streeter_new/BULKRNASEQ_AJ/PCA_RESULTS/SAMPLE1"
dir.create(output_dir, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

pca_condition <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = "condition") +
  ggtitle("PCA Plot - Bulk RNA-seq Samples by Condition") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

print(pca_condition)

ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_by_condition.pdf",
  plot = pca_condition,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 11. PCA plot colored by gene group
# ------------------------------------------------------------

pca_gene_group <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = "gene_group") +
  ggtitle("PCA Plot by Gene Group") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

print(pca_gene_group)

ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_by_gene_group.pdf",
  plot = pca_gene_group,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 12. PCA plot colored by status WT/KO
# ------------------------------------------------------------

pca_status <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = "status") +
  ggtitle("PCA Plot by WT/KO Status") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

print(pca_status)

ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_by_status.pdf",
  plot = pca_status,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 13. PCA plot colored by diet
# ------------------------------------------------------------

pca_diet <- plotPCA(vsd, intgroup = "diet") +
  ggtitle("PCA Plot by Diet") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

print(pca_diet)

ggsave(
  filename = "PCA_by_diet.pdf",
  plot = pca_diet,
  width = 8,
  height = 6
)

# ------------------------------------------------------------
# 14. Save processed objects for later DE analysis
# ------------------------------------------------------------

saveRDS(dds, "dds_object_before_DE.rds")
saveRDS(vsd, "vst_object_for_PCA.rds")
write.csv(sample_info, "sample_metadata_used_for_DESeq2.csv")

cat("PCA analysis completed successfully.\n")
```
